title: Alignment: SFT, RLHF, DPO/IPO/KTO/ORPO + DSA review
focus: RLHF three stages; reward hacking; why DPO is simpler.

items.label: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT)
interviewQuestions: Walk through the SFT objective — how is it different from pretraining?
How do you choose the SFT learning rate and number of epochs without overfitting on a small dataset?
How would you generate and filter synthetic SFT data using rejection sampling, model-based quality scoring, and semantic deduplication?

items.label: RLHF: reward model + PPO
interviewQuestions: Walk through the three stages of RLHF: SFT → reward model → PPO.
Why is reward hacking a problem in RLHF?
What implementation details matter in PPO-based RLHF: per-token KL, reward normalization, adaptive KL, dropout, and response-length handling?
Compare outcome reward models vs process reward models for reasoning-heavy tasks.

items.label: DPO and friends (IPO, KTO, ORPO)
interviewQuestions: Why is DPO simpler than PPO-based RLHF, and what does it sacrifice?
Compare DPO, IPO, KTO, and ORPO — when does each fit?
Walk through the tensors needed to implement DPO: chosen/rejected logprobs from the trainable model and the frozen reference model.
Why can deterministic preference labels weaken KL-style regularization, and how does IPO try to address that?
